draft.introduction: Malachi 3:1–4 prophesies a messenger who will prepare the way before the Lord, followed by the Lord’s sudden arrival at his temple. The Gospels identify John the Baptist as this messenger.

translation.text: Behold, I send my messenger, and he will prepare the way before me. And the Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple; and the messenger of the covenant in whom you delight, behold, he is coming. But who can endure the day of his coming? For he is like a refiner’s fire and like fullers’ soap.
translation.original_text: ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ἐξαποστέλλω τὸν ἄγγελόν μου καὶ ἐπιβλέψεται ὁδὸν πρὸ προσώπου μου

translator_notes.note: Malachi (“my messenger”) is the last book of the prophets in the Christian Old Testament. Mark’s Gospel opens by combining this verse with Isaiah 40:3 to frame John the Baptist as the prophesied forerunner, creating a bridge between the testaments.
